name: Parse walk-resolution parity
summary: Oracle-driven pairtools parse walk-policy resolution for supported non-all policies.
goal: Match pairtools parse walk-resolution behavior for mask, 5any, 5unique, 3any, and 3unique on deterministic SAM fixtures.
explicit_non_goals: Do not claim --walks-policy all unless oracle tests pass.
oracle_commands: pixi run pairtools parse --chroms-path tests/fixtures/walks/walks.chrom.sizes --assembly walks_test --min-mapq 10 --walks-policy 5unique --max-inter-align-gap 30 --max-molecule-size 750 --report-alignment-end 5 --add-columns mapq,pos5,pos3,cigar,read_len tests/fixtures/walks/simple_non_walk.sam
candidate_commands: target/debug/pairs-rs parse --chroms-path tests/fixtures/walks/walks.chrom.sizes --assembly walks_test --min-mapq 10 --walks-policy 5unique --max-inter-align-gap 30 --max-molecule-size 750 --report-alignment-end 5 --add-columns mapq,pos5,pos3,cigar,read_len tests/fixtures/walks/simple_non_walk.sam
acceptance_criteria: Walk-policy output matches pairtools oracle fixtures for mask, 5any, 5unique, 3any, and 3unique.
--walks-policy all fails loudly or is split into M056.
Parse output writing remains separate from walk policy selection.
known_risks: Full pairtools complex-walk behavior may need M056 for --walks-policy all.
